GitHub
Quick Setup

One command.
Running in minutes.

Agent PMO installs itself. One script handles .NET, the gh CLI, the dashboard, and the scheduled refresh. macOS, Linux, and Windows supported.

Copy. Paste. Done.

curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/MelbourneDeveloper/project_status/main/setup/setup.sh | bash

Works on macOS, Linux, and Windows. .NET SDK and gh CLI installed automatically if missing.

What you need

Prerequisites

The setup script handles most of this automatically. But here's what you're working with.

💻

macOS, Linux, or Windows

macOS 12+ (Homebrew recommended), Debian/Ubuntu/Fedora Linux, or Windows 10+ (winget/choco). The setup script detects your OS.

🔑

GitHub account with CLI auth

The dashboard uses gh to fetch PR and CI status. Run gh auth login once after install.

📁

Repos under ~/Documents/Code/

The dashboard scans this directory. If your repos live elsewhere, edit config.json after setup.

🟣

.NET SDK (auto-installed)

The dashboard is an F# script. The setup script installs .NET 8 via Homebrew (macOS) or apt/script (Linux).

Manual setup

Step by step

The one-liner does all of this automatically. But here's what's happening under the hood.

1

Clone the repo

Clone Agent PMO into your code directory.

git clone https://github.com/MelbourneDeveloper/project_status.git
cd project_status
2

Install .NET SDK

The dashboard is an F# script run via dotnet fsi. Install .NET 8+:

# macOS (Homebrew)
brew install dotnet

# Ubuntu / Debian
sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y dotnet-sdk-8.0

# Windows (winget)
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.8

# Or download from: https://dot.net
3

Install and authenticate gh CLI

The dashboard fetches PR and CI status via the GitHub CLI.

# macOS
brew install gh

# Ubuntu / Debian
sudo apt install gh

# Windows
winget install GitHub.cli

# Authenticate (all platforms)
gh auth login
4

Run the report manually

Verify everything works before setting up the scheduler.

dotnet fsi dashboard/repo-report.fsx
# Opens dashboard/repo-report.html

If you see your repos in the report, you're good to go.

5

Set up the scheduled refresh

The setup script handles this. Manual instructions:

# macOS — launchd (runs every 3 minutes)
cp com.agentpmo.repo-report.plist ~/Library/LaunchAgents/
launchctl load ~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.agentpmo.repo-report.plist

# Linux — cron job
echo "*/3 * * * * cd $(pwd) && dotnet fsi dashboard/repo-report.fsx" | crontab -

# Windows — Task Scheduler (PowerShell, admin)
# The setup.ps1 script handles this automatically
6

Install the enforcement skill (optional)

To standardise your other repos with the Claude skill:

make install-skill
# Symlinks enforce-repo-standards/ into ~/.claude/skills/

Then in any repo: tell Claude to run the enforce-repo-standards skill.

Configuration

Tailor it to your setup

Copy dashboard/config.example.json to dashboard/config.json to customise. 

{
  "scanPaths": [
    "~/Documents/Code",
    "~/Projects"
  ],
  "excludeRepos": [
    "archived-project",
    "vendor"
  ],
  "refreshIntervalSeconds": 180,
  "reportPath": "dashboard/repo-report.html",
  "github": {
    "fetchPRs": true,
    "fetchCI": true,
    "fetchReleases": true
  }
}
Multiple scan paths supported. If your repos live in different directories, list them all in scanPaths. The dashboard merges results from all paths.
What's next

You're set up. Now what?

📐

Standardise your repos

Run the enforce-repo-standards Claude skill on each of your repos. Give every project the same interface so agents can navigate them without help.

Learn about standards →
🌿

Set up worktrees for monorepos

Got a large codebase? Create worktrees so multiple agents can work simultaneously without conflicts.

Learn about worktrees →
🎭

Enable TMC

When TMC is available, enable it for cross-project orchestration. Agents coordinate, lock files, and avoid conflicts automatically.

Learn about TMC →

Questions? Issues? PRs?

Agent PMO is open source. Bugs, feature requests, and contributions are welcome on GitHub.

Open an Issue → View on GitHub
